message:
fcp-crypto/cose: enforce alg binding and crit handling on verify

`CoseToken::verify` previously trusted whatever bytes the caller passed,
binding to the algorithm only implicitly through the COSE Sig_structure
TBS encoding. That works while the verifier supports exactly one
algorithm, but two pieces of the RFC 8152 contract were silently
skipped:

* §4.4: "The application MUST verify that the cryptographic algorithm
  used is the one that the recipient expects." A token whose protected
  header carries `alg=ES256` (or no `alg` at all) was previously routed
  through Ed25519 verification anyway. Today the sig fails for the
  unrelated reason that the bytes don't verify; tomorrow, the moment a
  second key type joins the directory, the same code path becomes a
  downgrade vector.
* §3.1: "If a critical header is unknown to the recipient, the message
  MUST be rejected." We didn't even read the `crit` field, so a token
  could declare arbitrary semantics critical (e.g. a private-use header
  meaning "must check audit binding") and we'd ignore it while still
  treating the token as valid.

Harden `verify`:

1. Match `protected.header.alg` against
   `Algorithm::Assigned(iana::Algorithm::EdDSA)`. Reject `Some(other)`
   with `AlgorithmMismatch { expected, got }` and reject `None` with
   `MissingField("alg in protected header")`. The check runs before we
   touch the signature bytes.
2. Walk `protected.header.crit`. Allow `Assigned(Alg)` and
   `Assigned(Kid)` (the only labels we process). Anything else —
   assigned, private-use, or text — yields
   `UnsupportedCriticalHeader(label_debug)`.

`CryptoError` grows two variants for these (`AlgorithmMismatch`,
`UnsupportedCriticalHeader`); both implement Display via thiserror.
Sign path is unchanged.

Tests added in `cose::tests`:

* `verify_rejects_token_with_no_alg_in_protected_header`
* `verify_rejects_token_with_wrong_algorithm` — signs with the real
  Ed25519 key but advertises ES256, demonstrating that alg binding is
  now explicit (the underlying signature would otherwise verify because
  TBS covers the alg field).
* `verify_rejects_token_with_unknown_critical_header` — private-use
  label in `crit` triggers rejection.
* `verify_accepts_alg_kid_in_crit_when_understood` — labels we handle
  remain acceptable.

Verified locally: `cargo test -p fcp-crypto --lib` 496 passed, 0
failed; `cargo clippy -p fcp-crypto --lib -- -D warnings` clean;
`cargo test -p fcp-core --lib` 3911 passed (no consumer regressions).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -91,6 +91,20 @@ pub enum CryptoError {
     /// Missing required field.
     #[error("missing required field: {0}")]
     MissingField(String),
+
+    /// COSE protected-header algorithm did not match what the verifier expects.
+    #[error("algorithm mismatch: expected {expected}, got {got}")]
+    AlgorithmMismatch {
+        /// Expected algorithm description (e.g. `"EdDSA"`).
+        expected: &'static str,
+        /// Actual algorithm description from the protected header.
+        got: String,
+    },
+
+    /// COSE protected header lists a critical header label this verifier
+    /// does not understand (RFC 8152 §3.1 — message MUST be rejected).
+    #[error("unsupported critical header: {0}")]
+    UnsupportedCriticalHeader(String),
 }
 
 /// Result type alias for cryptographic operations.
